name: AMBAR Restaurant, Capitol Hill

notes: All 39 frozen rows were adjudicated: 15 exact matches, 15 normalized matches, five service/formulation variants, three stale extras, and one descriptionless parser artifact.
The current asserted catalog contains 104 food and nonalcoholic formulations across 267 source presentations; 35 current formulations match frozen rows and 69 were omitted from the frozen catalog.
The expired July 4 special and alcohol-only wine, beer, rakija, and cocktail rows are not part of the current food/nonalcoholic catalog.
Seventy-two current formulations have supported fixed first-party positive allergen signals and 32 remain accurately unavailable; no item-level may-contain claim was published or invented.
Fifteen frozen rows had allergen mismatches: 14 underreported supported allergens and Mushroom Pizza mixed an invented shellfish signal from oyster mushrooms with missing milk, wheat, and gluten.
GF, DF, NF, and SF are free-from/accommodation states; underlining means modification is required. Missing free-from icons were not inverted into positive contains claims.

Reviewed identity, current menu-surface selection, PDF legend semantics, stale-guide/current-menu joins, configurable at-home packages, same-name surface consolidation, alcohol exclusions, generic-crawler artifacts, target-only persistence, and final generated fields.

Nine focused catalog/reconciliation tests pass. The target-only repair preserved the SHA-256 of every non-AMBAR restaurant at 34555ea87f56cfe21ab6ecbd9ab146ff15d661910685b4511e3a2fb291fd0dbb and is idempotent at target hash e67c788e1be5ee4660030d9f7bfdbcfdebf94b93cd8de9eb034571e158bd4ccf.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 403 for the ledger fetch user-agent; the same current first-party pages were then captured successfully with curl.
notes: Cloudflare selectively rejected the audit tool user-agent; this was a transport restriction, not source unavailability.

findings.summary: The frozen generic crawl mixed stale ordering content with broad site pages, emitted one descriptionless child row, retained three stale formulations, and omitted 69 current Capitol Hill formulations.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt 104 current food/nonalcoholic formulations from the current May/June 2026 documents and restaurant-linked ordering surface; removed one artifact and three stale rows.

findings.summary: Fifteen frozen rows disagreed with current first-party evidence: 14 underreported fixed allergens and Mushroom Pizza both falsely mapped oyster mushroom to shellfish and omitted milk, wheat, and gluten.
findings.resolution: Applied fixed current description signals and narrowly used underlined modification markers; removed the oyster-mushroom shellfish false positive and added only supported allergens.

findings.summary: AMBAR allergy symbols describe GF/DF/NF/SF free-from or accommodation states, with underlining for required modification; treating missing symbols as a contains matrix would create unsupported claims.
findings.resolution: Preserved the visual legend semantics, did not invert absent icons, and did not create unsupported cross-contact or negative safety claims.

findings.summary: The allergy PDFs were modified in January 2026, while current food menus were created in May/June 2026 and include additions/replacements.
findings.resolution: Joined allergy-guide semantics only to unchanged matching formulations and used current menu descriptions for newer rows; stale guide-only names were not restored.

findings.summary: The frozen record displayed only Washington, DC instead of the current Capitol Hill street address.
findings.resolution: Set the display address to 523 8th St SE, Washington, DC 20003 and the guide to the current Capitol Hill menu index.

repairs.summary: Replaced the polluted 39-row crawl with 104 current Capitol Hill food/nonalcoholic formulations and 267 source presentations; corrected 15 frozen allergen results and removed one parser artifact plus three stale rows.

notes: Current address: 523 8th St SE, Washington, DC 20003.
The a-la-carte, lunch, dinner, dessert, drinks, and happy-hour PDFs carry May 2026 creation dates; the current brunch PDF carries a June 30, 2026 creation date.
At-home packages are configurable and include a variable dessert; only fixed published components were retained as contains signals.
No safe dedicated audit worktree exists because the checkout remains dirty, so no checkpoint commit was created.
